title: About Sai Tarrun Pitta
text: Sai Tarrun Pitta is a Full-Stack Software Engineer with 3+ years of experience designing and delivering scalable backend systems, distributed cloud platforms, and AI-enabled applications. He is skilled in building high-volume payment platforms, cloud infrastructure, RAG retrieval pipelines, and secure production systems, with a strong focus on reliability, performance optimization, and security-first engineering. He is currently available for full-time software engineer roles.

title: Contact Information
text: Sai Tarrun Pitta can be reached at email: saitarrunpitta@gmail.com. LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/saitarrunpitta. GitHub: github.com/saitarrun. Portfolio website: https://saitarrun.dev. Phone: +1 (657) 751-9260.

title: Job Availability
text: Sai Tarrun Pitta is currently available for work and actively seeking full-time software engineer positions. He is open to backend, full-stack, and AI/ML engineering roles. He is based in California and has experience working both in the US and India.

title: Pacific Life - Software Engineer Intern
text: Sai worked at Pacific Life as a Software Engineer Intern from May 2025 to December 2025 in Newport Beach, California. He automated 98% of claims triage workflows by architecting a serverless ML/LLM service using AWS Lambda, Amazon API Gateway, TensorFlow, and LangChain multi-agent orchestration. He reduced claims processing time by 75% (from 3 hours to 45 minutes) and workflow failures by 30% by implementing human-in-the-loop validation checkpoints. He accelerated policy lookup by 98% and boosted claims processor productivity by 50% by engineering a secure RAG pipeline with LLM embeddings, Pinecone semantic search, source-cited responses, and access-controlled retrieval. He built a TypeScript API aggregation layer with Redis distributed caching, reducing dashboard load time from 4 seconds to 2 seconds (50% improvement) and API p99 latency from 300ms to 210ms. He integrated ML inference into an event-driven Kafka pipeline achieving under 100ms latency for risk updates on 5,000+ weekly claims with 99.99% reliability.

title: Accenture - Software Engineer
text: Sai worked at Accenture as a Software Engineer from July 2022 to June 2024 in Hyderabad, India. He scaled a gRPC-based transaction microservice to process 2 million+ daily transactions with 20,000 transactions per second peak throughput and 99.99% uptime using Protocol Buffers, idempotency keys, and Redis distributed locking. He led AWS cloud infrastructure optimization using Amazon EKS auto-scaling and Amazon ElastiCache connection pooling, reducing compute costs by 50% and cloud spending by 35% (saving $15,000 per month). He optimized SQL queries across distributed transaction systems using composite indexes and query refactoring, reducing latency by 60% and database CPU usage by 73%. He designed a reliability architecture with Amazon CloudWatch, Prometheus, and Splunk monitoring pipelines with automated alerts, reducing mean time to recovery (MTTR) by 25 times for business-critical services.

title: Cognizant - Software Engineer
text: Sai worked at Cognizant as a Software Engineer from May 2021 to April 2022 in Hyderabad, India. He improved PostgreSQL query performance and resolved lock contention in Spring Boot services, reducing API latency by 52% (from 2.5 seconds to 1.2 seconds) and lowering p99 latency by 30%. He engineered an ML churn prediction system using scikit-learn and TensorFlow with React dashboards across 50,000 customers, achieving 12% churn reduction and 87% forecast accuracy. He built REST APIs exposing real-time churn predictions for 5,000+ customers, enabling proactive retention operations. He implemented RBAC and input validation, remediating 40+ high-severity vulnerabilities for a clean security audit. He standardized CI/CD with Docker and GitHub Actions, reducing release lead time by 50% and enabling daily deployments.

title: California State University, Fullerton - Master of Science Computer Science
text: Sai is pursuing a Master of Science in Computer Science at California State University, Fullerton from August 2024 to May 2026. His coursework includes Advanced Algorithms, Cloud Computing, System Design, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, and Distributed Systems. He is focused on deep learning, security-first cloud architecture, and high-performance computing.

title: IEEE Publication: Hardware Trojan Detection
text: Sai published a peer-reviewed IEEE conference paper titled 'Hardware Trojan Detection with Machine Learning and Power Side-Channels: A Post-Deployment Analysis' at IEEE CNS 2025. The research explores using power side-channel analysis and Random Forest models to detect dormant hardware Trojans in integrated circuits. By analyzing power consumption data from an Artix FPGA running AES-128, the model achieved 98% detection accuracy while reducing computational costs and improving noise robustness. Co-authored with Ashwin Koshy John, Jaya Dofe, and Jai Gopal Pandey.

title: LLM-Powered Knowledge Retrieval Platform
text: Sai built a distributed full-stack LLM-Powered Knowledge Retrieval Platform using LangChain, Pinecone, FastAPI, React, and TypeScript. The platform features semantic search, vector indexing, and NLP-driven query matching. It improved information retrieval accuracy by 25% and reduced user search time by 40% by leveraging RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architectures.

title: Production Portfolio & Personal Website
text: Sai's production-grade portfolio is built with React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript, deployed on Vercel. It features an advanced UI with Three.js 3D backgrounds (Orange Smoke, Particle Field, Grid Plane), Framer Motion animations, and Lenis smooth scrolling. The site includes a custom AI chatbot using a RAG architecture with OpenRouter API integration. It follows high code quality standards with TypeScript strict mode, ESLint security rules, and automated CI/CD via GitHub Actions.

title: Event-Driven E-Commerce Modernization
text: Sai architected a monolith-to-microservices migration using the Strangler pattern, REST APIs, and event-driven design with Node.js, Kafka, and PostgreSQL. This project enables zero-downtime canary deployments and scalable order, payment, and inventory workflows, leveraging Docker and Kubernetes for container orchestration in high-traffic environments.

title: Brain Tumor Detection Pipeline
text: Sai built an end-to-end brain tumor detection pipeline using TensorFlow, Apache Spark, GCP, and OpenCV. This distributed ML pipeline processes MRI scan data at scale, leveraging Spark for big data handling and GCP for cloud-native inference to achieve clinical-grade tumor detection accuracy.

title: Engineering Philosophy
text: Sai's engineering philosophy centers on 'Performance by Design' and 'Security as a Default.' He prioritizes architectural reliability (99.99% uptime), observability (MTTR reduction), and performance optimization (reducing p99 latencies). He believes in building systems that are not just functional but also scalable, maintainable, and cost-efficient.

title: Comprehensive Technical Skillset
text: Sai is proficient in Python, Java, Go, TypeScript, and SQL. His backend expertise includes Spring Boot, Node.js, FastAPI, and gRPC. On the frontend, he specializes in React, Next.js, and Three.js. His infrastructure skills cover AWS, GCP, Docker, Kubernetes, and Terraform. He is also highly skilled in AI/ML tools like TensorFlow, LangChain, Pinecone, and RAG systems.

title: AI/ML and RAG Expertise
text: Sai has deep expertise in building RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems and agentic AI workflows. He uses LangChain for multi-agent orchestration, Pinecone for vector search, and LLM embeddings for semantic retrieval. His experience includes automating claims triage and building knowledge retrieval platforms with human-in-the-loop validation.

title: Cloud Infrastructure and DevOps
text: Sai is expert in AWS and GCP cloud architectures. He has experience with serverless computing (AWS Lambda), container orchestration (EKS, Kubernetes), and infrastructure as code (Terraform). He specializes in cloud cost optimization, having saved $15,000/month by optimizing EKS and ElastiCache usage.

title: Security, Compliance and Identity
text: Sai implements high security standards including SOC 2, ISO 27001, and OWASP. He is experienced in RBAC, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, and JWT for secure identity management. He has a track record of remediating high-severity vulnerabilities and securing transaction systems.
